trivia_bullets.text: Malefic Scythe is an M21 original — first and only printing as of May 2026. It is an uncommon black Artifact — Equipment released July 3, 2020. [Scryfall]

The soul counter mechanic is self-scaling: the Scythe enters with one counter, grows by one each time the equipped creature dies, and grants +1/+1 per counter. With cheap equip (1 mana), it functions as a death-profiting repeatable buff that becomes stronger through natural board attrition. EDHREC rank 18,060 suggests light Commander adoption, primarily in sacrifice-themed black decks. No flavor text. [Scryfall]

Art by Joe Slucher. No human figure appears — the scythe is venerated as an altar piece inside a stone shrine lit by four candle sconces. The framing as a reliquary (weapon-as-relic) is a deliberate inversion of Equipment cards that normally show the weapon in use. Slucher's object-study approach here is notable for an Equipment card, where action-figure illustration is the genre default. [Scryfall]

Soul counters as a named counter type appear on a small number of cards in MTG history. The Malefic Scythe is one of the few Equipment cards to use a named accumulating counter as its growth engine rather than +1/+1 counters placed on the creature directly — a mechanical distinction that makes counter-doubling effects (Doubling Season, Vorinclex) not apply to the buff calculation. [Scryfall]
